typescript.name: Local auth with OS keychain (keytar)
typescript.context: For single-user desktop apps. Store credentials in OS keychain. No server needed.

typescript.name: OAuth2 with PKCE (via system browser)
typescript.context: For apps that connect to a web service. Opens system browser for auth, receives token via localhost redirect.
typescript.when: Apps connected to a web API or cloud service

rust.name: OS keychain (keyring crate)
rust.context: Store credentials in macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, or Linux Secret Service.

csharp.name: Windows Credential Manager (.NET)
csharp.context: Native Windows credential storage. Use CredentialManager NuGet package.

csharp.name: MSAL (Microsoft Authentication Library)
csharp.context: Azure AD / Microsoft Entra ID integration. Best for enterprise SSO.

default.name: OS keychain integration
default.context: Use the operating system's native credential storage. Secure, no server required.

typescript.name: Electron + React
typescript.context: Most mature desktop framework for web technologies. Large ecosystem. Higher memory usage. Best tooling support.

typescript.name: Tauri + React
typescript.context: Rust-based alternative to Electron. Much smaller binaries, lower memory. Newer, smaller ecosystem.
typescript.when: Performance-sensitive apps or if bundle size matters

rust.name: Tauri
rust.context: Native Rust backend with web frontend. Small binaries, low memory, system-native feel.

csharp.name: WPF (.NET)
csharp.context: Windows-only but mature and full-featured. Best for Windows-targeted business tools.

csharp.name: MAUI (.NET)
csharp.context: Cross-platform .NET UI. Works on Windows, macOS, iOS, Android. Newer, still maturing.

default.name: Electron (cross-platform) or Tauri (lightweight)
default.context: Electron for maximum compatibility and ecosystem. Tauri for smaller, faster apps.

default.name: Platform-native installers (DMG, MSI, AppImage)
default.context: DMG for macOS, MSI/NSIS for Windows, AppImage/deb for Linux. Users expect native formats.

default.name: Portable executables
default.context: No installation required. Good for internal tools or USB distribution.
default.when: Internal tools, no admin rights environments

default.name: Built-in framework updater (electron-updater / tauri-updater)
default.context: Downloads and installs updates automatically. Built into each framework.
default.when: Most desktop apps distributed outside app stores

default.name: Manual update notification
default.context: App checks for new version and shows a link. Simpler but relies on user action.

default.name: SQLite (local)
default.context: Embedded database, no server needed. Perfect for single-user desktop apps. Fast, reliable, zero-config.
default.when: Most desktop apps with local data

default.name: Supabase (cloud PostgreSQL)
default.context: For desktop apps that sync data to the cloud or across devices.
default.when: Multi-device sync or cloud backup needed
